title: Writing - Part 1: Gaming and Screen Time
description: Your class has been discussing the effects of excessive screen time and video gaming on young people.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the effects in your notes. You should explain which effect you consider most serious, giving reasons for your opinion.

effects: Reduced physical activity and health problems
Effects on social skills and real-world relationships
opinions: Gaming can become addictive and take over young people's lives.
Screen use before bed significantly disrupts sleep patterns.
Time spent on screens is time not spent developing real social skills.

structure.introduction: Introduce the issue of screen time and gaming and the two effects you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first effect (e.g., reduced physical activity) and the health consequences.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second effect (e.g., impact on sleep) and why sleep matters.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both effects and state which you consider most serious.

content.modelAnswer:
The amount of time young people spend on screens has increased dramatically in recent years, leading to growing concern among parents, teachers and health professionals. While digital technology offers many benefits, excessive use can cause significant harm. This essay will examine the health consequences of reduced physical activity and the damaging effect of screens on sleep.

Spending hours sitting in front of a screen inevitably reduces the time available for physical activity. Children and teenagers who are inactive are at greater risk of obesity, poor posture and cardiovascular problems. Physical activity is also vital for mental health, and its absence can contribute to increased levels of stress and depression. The sedentary lifestyle associated with heavy screen use is one of the major public health concerns of the twenty-first century.

Equally important is the negative impact on sleep. Research consistently shows that using screens in the evening, especially smartphones and tablets, delays the release of melatonin and makes it harder to fall asleep. Teenagers who sleep less than the recommended amount perform worse at school, have lower mood and are more susceptible to illness.

In conclusion, while both effects are serious, I believe the impact on sleep quality is the most immediate concern. Without adequate rest, every aspect of a young person's life suffers, from academic performance to emotional wellbeing. Parents and schools should set clear guidelines on screen use in the hours before bedtime.
